claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'm asking about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know! I'll help you assess eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking: **How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

Next, I need to ask: **Do all three children live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for all three children? By this I mean, are you their parent or the main person looking after them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm their parent and I'm responsible for all three of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Let me ask: **Are any of the children currently in care (such as local authority care) or have they been in hospital for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in care or have been in hospital.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now provide you with a determination for each of your children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the summary:

- **Alex (age 3)**: ✅ Eligible
- **Blake (age 8)**: ✅ Eligible  
- **Charlie (age 2)**: ✅ Eligible

All three children meet the eligibility criteria because they are:
- Under 16 years old
- Living with you in the UK
- You are their parent and responsible for them
- Not in care or hospital
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for all three children. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent and responsible for them, they are not in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent and responsible for them, they are not in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent and responsible for them, they are not in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16 years old, live with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is their parent and responsible for them, none are in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. All eligibility criteria are met for each child.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent and responsible for them, they are not in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are their parent and responsible for them, they are not in care or hospital, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
